libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Preserve or add JVM test arguments and native-image build arguments for: --add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED, --add-opens=java.base/java.net=ALL-UNNAMED, and --add-opens=java.base/sun.nio.ch=ALL-UNNAMED. Use org.conscrypt.Conscrypt.checkAvailability() before provider-dependent assertions, prefer loopback sockets or in-memory crypto/certificate tests, and avoid external network services.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8879 [Automation] native-image run fails for org.conscrypt:conscrypt-openjdk-uber:1.4.1; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: The uber artifact embeds native libraries and extracts/loads them at runtime; failures may still occur if Native Image cannot load or include the packaged JNI library/resource correctly.
Version 1.4.1 is old relative to JDK 25, so native-image runtime failures may reflect library/JDK compatibility rather than missing test setup.
Do not add platform-classifier Conscrypt artifacts alongside conscrypt-openjdk-uber; the uber JAR is intended to avoid extra OS-specific dependency setup.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Conscrypt OpenJDK uber has no transitive Maven dependencies and already bundles the platform native libraries, so no dependency or Docker setup is needed; however meaningful socket/provider coverage on modern JDKs needs module opens for Conscrypt's internal access to JDK socket/file-descriptor internals.
